title: People Love to Hate This Fake Private Equity Guy
description: Johnny Hilbrant Partridge has found social media fame as PE Guy, a character who embodies obnoxious entitlement.
author: Guy Trebay

title: SpaceX Has $30 Billion Deal to Provide Google With A.I. Computing Power
description: Elon Musk’s rocket company said Google would pay it $920 million a month, as it prepared for its initial public offering.
author: Kate Conger

title: Reid Hoffman to Leave Microsoft’s Board of Directors
description: The LinkedIn co-founder was a key bridge to Microsoft’s relationship with OpenAI, but he also came with some baggage.
author: Karen Weise and Cade Metz

title: Why the SpaceX IPO Will Affect Your 401(k), Like It or Not
description: Elon Musk’s rocket company, on the cusp of the largest initial public offering ever, will soon end up in index funds after rule changes by Nasdaq and other index providers.
author: Mike Isaac and Maureen Farrell

title: Hot I.P.O. Summer + What Is A.I. Doing to Math? + HatGPT
description: “We are on track to see what might be the three biggest I.P.O.s of all time.”
author: Kevin Roose, Casey Newton, Whitney Jones, Rachel Cohn, Vjeran Pavic, Chris Wood, Dan Powell, Elisheba Ittoop, Marion Lozano and Rowan Niemisto

title: From Cow-Milking Robots to Weed-Zapping Lasers, Farmers Are Embracing A.I.
description: We asked three farmers to tell us how new technology is revolutionizing the way they work.
author: Coralie Kraft and Spencer Lowell

title: Wary of U.S., Carney Bets on AI Strategy for Canada
description: The country on Thursday released a national artificial intelligence strategy that focuses on building its sovereign capability and protecting consumers.
author: Vjosa Isai

title: Gwynne Shotwell, Elon Musk’s No. 2 at SpaceX, Is the Company’s Steady Hand
description: Gwynne Shotwell, the president and chief operating officer, is the adult-in-the-room foil to Mr. Musk as SpaceX prepares for a blockbuster initial public offering.
author: Ryan Mac

title: How LinkedIn Found Its Social Platform Era
description: Celebrities and paid influencers have transformed the career-focused platform, which for some users, has become a job unto itself.
author: Isabella Kwai

title: What Are A.I. Agents Actually Doing?
description: A San Francisco start-up called Arena found that people are most likely to use A.I. agents on the job, particularly if they are in the tech industry.
author: Cade Metz

title: Can These ChatGPT Ads Make You Love A.I.?
description: A campaign for ChatGPT relies on heartwarming retro vibes to sell a product that has become a source of concern for most Americans.
author: Steven Kurutz

title: Who’s Excited for SpaceX’s I.P.O.? Space Nerds.
description: Few are as delighted by the pending market debut as the vibrant community of space enthusiasts who want to push discovery of the final frontier.
author: Sheera Frenkel

title: ‘Teachers Are Going to Hate It’: How Social Media Apps Hooked Teens at School
description: Internal documents show how tech giants grabbed children’s attention throughout the day, a strategy that schools say has undermined education.
author: Jennifer Valentino-DeVries

title: The Do’s and Don’ts of Buying Used Tech Gadgets
description: With a shortage of some components driving up costs of consumer electronics, refurbished devices are an attractive alternative to buying new.
author: Brian X. Chen

title: The Small-Business Owners Managing Whole Armies of A.I. Employees
description: When you turn A.I. agents loose on your finances, email and customers, what could possibly go wrong?
author: Clive Thompson

title: SpaceX IPO to Be Largest Ever at $135 Share Price
description: The $135 share price means Elon Musk’s rocket maker is poised to exceed the 2019 initial public offering of Saudi Aramco in both valuation and money raised.
author: Ryan Mac

title: Joby Demonstrated its Air Taxi in Manhattan, but You Can’t Fly in It Yet
description: Aviation start-ups and the Trump administration want to replace helicopters with electric aircraft, but the new vehicles still have to pass arduous tests before the public can use them.
author: Niraj Chokshi

title: Powerful A.I. Super PACs Duel Over the Midterms: ‘This Is a War’
description: One super PAC is allied with Anthropic. The other is tied to OpenAI. They’re both spending millions to influence this year’s elections.
author: Theodore Schleifer

title: What’s Next for Blue Origin After Rocket Explosion
description: Jeff Bezos was gaining ground on Elon Musk’s SpaceX and Starlink. Thursday’s rocket explosion on a launchpad creates a major setback.
author: Karen Weise

title: Interesting Times: Why Are We Still Driving?
description: Confronting the weirdness of a Waymo future.
author: Ross Douthat and Sophia Alvarez Boyd
